title: Shinji and Hideaki Anno
wikitext:
[]: Shinji and Hideaki Anno             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Wed Feb 27, 2013 7:30 pm How much do you think does Shinjey resemble Hideaki Anno? And in which scenes do you think Anno talks the most about himself? (Like those scenes in End of Eva that apparently don´t make any sense, but if you read interviews with Anno...)

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Feb 27, 2013 8:15 pm Mentally, a lot. There are a lot of interviews on the subject.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Wed Feb 27, 2013 8:33 pm What I always think is "Did Anno really end up like Shinji when he was 14? Or did this happen at a different time?" Of course it is a story, but I ask myself how much is actually based on real events. And would Anno really pilot the EVA so that Rei doesn´t do it? That is Anno talking pretty good about himself.

[]:             Quote Post by evangelist » Wed Feb 27, 2013 8:42 pm They both have similar characteristics and traits so I wouldn't be surprised if Anno's own psychological and personal issues helped shape the Shinji that we know of.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Thu Feb 28, 2013 12:29 am Shinji is literally the 35 year old Anno that made the series inserted into an anime. When asked about the subject he said Shinji is the current me.

[]:             Quote Post by SimplyMason0 » Thu Feb 28, 2013 1:24 am It sounds to me that Anno is pretty much an adult Shinji. Shinji's issues could apply to an adult where one is too scared of human interaction. Probably the scene like where Shinji was too scared to hug Misato or hold Asuka. I think the part in EoE where Shinji and Rei talked about dreams says something about what Anno was like since thats the main point of the show. Since the show tackles otakus, I think Anno used that kind of escapism to run away but left that stuff out with Shinji's character.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Thu Feb 28, 2013 10:27 am Shinji is just a more feminine looking Anno.

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Thu Feb 28, 2013 3:47 pm Yeah, to all intents and purposes, Shinji is a 14-year old, adorable Anno. The interesting thing about Shinji is that he is one of the best self-insert characters ever made. Most self-inserts tend to be massive Mary Sues who can trump any obstacle and are good at everything. Shinji is the complete opposite, a character who is convinced that they have no self-worth (just as Anno did at the time of writing NGE) and who struggles to get through everyday life without just curling up in a ball and hoping it all goes away. If Shinji were a tabletop RPG character, and Anno were the player who made him, the Dungeon Master would say "You've made a very underpowered character, I think you should give yourself a better image" to which he would reply "No, it's fine, he's already a better person than me, carry on". If Anno weren't so beardy (and also now happily married) I'd give the poor guy a hug. Fortunately I don't think he needs it anymore. Watching Rebuild, you can tell Shinji is a stronger, more confident character, showing that Anno has definitely cheered up a bit.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Feb 28, 2013 4:02 pm Fireball wrote: Shinji is just a more feminine looking Anno.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Feb 28, 2013 6:37 pm Chuckman wrote: Deep down, Anno just wants to be pretty.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Thu Feb 28, 2013 7:30 pm Do you think that creating the series he wanted to show some specific person how he is?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Feb 28, 2013 11:39 pm kuribo-04 wrote: Do you think that creating the series he wanted to show some specific person how he is?

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Thu Feb 28, 2013 11:47 pm Ornette wrote: Read this: http://wiki.evageeks.org/Statements_by_Evangelion_Staff#Hideaki_Anno:_What_were_we_trying_to_make_here.3F

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Mar 01, 2013 12:40 am kuribo-04 wrote: Ah yes, I read that a long time ago. But who was he trying to show how he is? Thanks for showing me this document again. It's very interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by Practice » Fri Mar 01, 2013 5:44 am Shinji is very clearly a represention of Anno. The reason Eva is by far my favorite anime (the entire reason I got into anime) is because so much of Shinji/Anno's struggle with depression resonates with my own. Not only that, but Evangelion actually helped me realize how far I've come. People always argue about why Eva is so loved/hated or why it's had such a massive impact, whether it be the "deconstruction of the genre" or what not. In my opinion the reason it's so successful is that it's simply Anno pouring his being out into a story; at least that's how it strikes me. "It is a production where my only thought was to burn my feelings into film." That right there is why I consider Eva to be the greatest piece of art I've ever experienced.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Fri Mar 01, 2013 6:21 am Ornette wrote: Well, since we don't know what Anno was thinking back then, and we only have scraps and disjointed pieces of random interviews or statements, that particular passage seems to link him to Shinji, and that he wasn't trying to create a series for any one particular person.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Fri Mar 01, 2013 6:28 am Practice wrote: People always argue about why Eva is so loved/hated or why it's had such a massive impact, whether it be the "deconstruction of the genre" or what not. In my opinion the reason it's so successful is that it's simply Anno pouring his being out into a story; at least that's how it strikes me. "It is a production where my only thought was to burn my feelings into film." That right there is why I consider Eva to be the greatest piece of art I've ever experienced.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Fri Mar 01, 2013 6:47 am Dream wrote: While there's no doubt that Anno is by far the one who poured the most effort, craft, blood and tears into the production (plus the extremely hard thing to do of "burn one's feelings into film") he's far from the only person responsible for Evangelion's diamond quality. Also, i think Mason0 brings a good point in noting that many of the dilemmas Shinji finds himself involved in tend to involve stuff of a more adult nature. It's certainly not stuff you'd think a 14 year old should deal with, at least @Kuribo: I fail to see what's bad (or more accurately, surprising) about the fact that a not very social, lonely artistic genius tried to "impress" (no idea what that means actually) a couple young women he worked with.

[]:             Quote Post by Oenara » Fri Mar 01, 2013 5:57 pm Chuckman wrote: Deep down, Anno just wants to be pretty.

[]:             Quote Post by Practice » Fri Mar 01, 2013 7:15 pm Dream wrote: While there's no doubt that Anno is by far the one who poured the most effort, craft, blood and tears into the production (plus the extremely hard thing to do of "burn one's feelings into film") he's far from the only person responsible for Evangelion's diamond quality.

And in which scenes do you think Anno talks the most about himself? (Like those scenes in End of Eva that apparently don´t make any sense, but if you read interviews with Anno...)

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » W
